caption: Figure 1. Regression result for ferrite, apparent density (= 1/regression coefficient $r_i$ ) compared to density of element and literature values (see table 3)
paper_title: Evaluation of the density of steels
citation_sentences: Model II. The result for the case where the solubilities of Cr and Mn in cementite were computed with Thermo-Calc is given in table 3. Figure 1 shows the inverse of the regression coefficients $r_i$ (apparent density) with error-bars for 95% probability compared to the densities of the elements and literature values. Al, Nb, P, and Ti are insignificant in the amounts present in this study. Cu and Mo increase $\rho_{\text{ferrite}}$ ; C, Cr, Mn, Ni, S, Si, and V reduce $\rho_{\text{ferrite}}$ . The model has $R^2 = 0.994$ , a maximum deviation of 4.2 kg/m $^3$ and an average deviation of 1.4 kg/m $^3$ . The literature values match the present regression results closely for Cr, Cu, Fe, Mn, Mo (some deviation), Ni, S, and Si; the value for V deviates. The elemental densities match the regression result for Cr, Cu, Fe, Mn, Mo (some deviation), and S. Deviations are found for Ni, Si, and V.

caption: Figure 2. Density of cementite with structures Pnma and Pbnm as a function of Cr- respectively Mn-content
paper_title: Evaluation of the density of steels
citation_sentences: (i) density of cementite computed separately, see text and figure 2; (iii) covalent bonds
for $C_{\mathrm{Mn}}^{\mathrm{wt}}$ in cementite above 0.12 (there were no mixed cases), figure 2.

caption: Figure 3. Regression result for austenite, apparent density (=1/regression coefficient $r_{i}$ ) compared to density of element and literature values (see table 4)
paper_title: Evaluation of the density of steels
citation_sentences: Austenitic steels. 64 batches of steel have been studied by use of equation (4) with x = austenite, including the mass content of TiC as a variable. Solutes in TiC were neglected. The results are shown in table 4 and figure 3. TiC, C, Cr, Mn, N, Si, and Ti reduce $\rho_{austenite}$ and Co, Cu, Mo, and Ni increase $\rho_{austenite}$ . The value of $R^{2}$ is 0.975. The maximum deviation is 10.4 kg/m $^{3}$ , the average deviation is 2.6 kg/m $^{3}$ .
